verses.commentary: Submission is commanded for everyone. All authority comes from God; He establishes rulers. This does not mean all rulers are good, but all are under God's sovereignty. Government is divinely ordained.
Rebellion against government is rebellion against God's order. Judgment follows disobedience. This presupposes government functioning within its God-given role.
Those who do right need not fear authority. Doing what is right brings commendation. This describes government functioning properly.
Their purpose: the good of society. The sword symbolizes authority to punish, including capital punishment. They execute God's wrath on evildoers. This is God's restraint on evil through government.
Conscience recognizes God's authority behind government. Christian obedience is not merely pragmatic but moral. We submit because it is right, not just expedient.

Paying taxes is an act of submission. Authorities are God's servants (leitourgoi, λειτουργοί, public servants). They devote themselves to governing. Funding government is supporting God's ordained institution.
Taxes and revenue are financial obligations. Respect (phobos, φόβος, fear) and honor (timē, τιμή) are attitudes of esteem. Government deserves both material support and respectful attitudes.
The one debt that remains: love. Love is an ongoing obligation. Love fulfills the law because it naturally does what the law commands. Love is the summary of ethics.
Whatever other command expands the principle. All are summed up (anakephalaioō, ἀνακεφαλαιόω, brought under one heading) in love your neighbor (Leviticus 19:18). Love is the fulfillment of law.
Love is inherently protective and benevolent. Fulfillment (plērōma, πλήρωμα) means full satisfaction of law's demands. Love accomplishes what law requires.
Wake up from spiritual sleep. Salvation here is future: final redemption. It is nearer than when we first believed. Time is short; live accordingly.

Day is Christ's return and eternal day. The transition is near. Put aside (apotithēmi, ἀποτίθημι, take off) deeds of darkness. Put on (endyō, ἐνδύω, clothe yourself with) armor of light. Lifestyle must match the approaching day.
Three pairs of vices: carousing and drunkenness (excess), sexual immorality and debauchery (lust), dissension and jealousy (relational sin). These belong to darkness, not light.

This is union with Christ, living in His character. Do not make provision (pronoian, πρόνοιαν, forethought) for the flesh. Do not plan to satisfy sinful desires. This verse converted Augustine. Christ, not flesh, must dominate our lives.
